data.title: Blue Owl, Mirion Technologies, EQT recommended as longs at Sohn Conf
data.description:
Mohammed Anjarwala, managing director of Advent Global Opportunities, recommended buying shares of Blue Owl Capital(NYSE:OWL), a manager of private credit funds.

Anjarwala made the recommendation at the Sohn Investment Conference for charity in New York on Wednesday. The company has a growing, annuity-like revenue stream, he said. He has a $36 a share price target based on 25x next-12 month p/e.

Dan Dreyfus, founder and chief investment officer of Bornite Capital, recommends Mirion Technologies (NYSE:MIR) as the U.S. adopts more nuclear power to keep up with demand for electricity. Mirion provides radiation detection to keep people safe.

Ryan Packard, managing partner and chief investment officer of Hiddenite Capital Partners recommended Comfort Systems (NYSE:FIX) as a key part of the industrial supply chain.

Salil Sharma, managing director, Kathmandu division of Leucadia Asset Management, recommended EQT Corp. (NYSE:EQT) as he's bullish on natural gas. 

--reported by Rob Williams in New York.

data.title: Cisco climbs as financial outlook, RPOs surpass estimates
data.description:
Cisco Systems (NASDAQ:CSCO) reported a stronger outlook than the market expected when it released its third quarter fiscal 2025 financial results after the bell on Wednesday.

As a result, shares climbed 3.6% during early post-market trading.

For the period ended April 26, Cisco reported adjusted earnings per share of $0.96, which was more than the consensus estimate of $0.92. GAAP EPS was $0.62, which equaled the estimate of $0.62.

Revenue for the third quarter totaled $14.1B, versus the estimate of $14.06B. Product orders were up 20%, while AI infrastructure orders surpassed $600M.

Remaining performance obligations increased 7% to $41.7B, which surpassed the consensus of $40.48B. Fifty-one percent of this is expected to materialize into revenue over the next 12 months.

Looking ahead, Cisco expects fourth quarter revenue to range from $14.5B to $16.7B, with a midpoint above the estimate of $14.51B. Adjusted EPS is projected to range from $0.96 to $0.98 which was also more than the estimate of $0.95. GAAP EPS is expected to range from $0.62 to $0.67.

Cisco now expects full-year revenue to range from $56.5B to $56.7B, compared to prior guidance of $56B to $56.5B. The new midpoint was well above an analysts' consensus of $56.4B.

"Cisco once again had strong quarterly results with clear demand for our technologies," said Cisco CEO Chuck Robbins. "The momentum we are seeing with AI is fueled by the power of our secure networking portfolio, our trusted global partnerships, and the value we bring to our customers."

The company also declared a quarterly divided of $0.41 per common share, as well as completion of the acquisition of SnapAttack, which offers a threat detection and engineering program. 

On Tuesday, Cisco unveiled a new partnership with Saudi Arabia's newly formed AI company, HUMAIN.

The San Jose-based company focuses on providing networking products for the communications and information technology industry. It also develops software, cybersecurity solutions and artificial intelligence architecture.

data.title: Boot Barn GAAP EPS of $1.22 misses by $0.02, revenue of $453.75M misses by $4.65M
data.description:
• Boot Barn press release (NYSE:BOOT): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.22 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $453.75M (+16.8% Y/Y) misses by $4.65M.
  • Same store sales increased 6.0%, with retail store same store sales increasing 5.5% and e-commerce same store sales increasing 9.8%.
  • The Company opened 21 new stores, bringing its total store count to 459 as of the quarter end.
  • The Company’s Board of Directors has authorized the Company to repurchase up to $200 million of its common stock.

data.title: Karooooo reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Karooooo press release (NASDAQ:KARO): Karooooo’s adjusted EPS increased 39% to ZAR9.48 in Q4 2025.
  • Karooooo subscription revenue increased 16% to ZAR1,086 million in Q4 2025 (Q4 2024: ZAR935 million), and operating profit increased 30% to ZAR385 million (Q4 2024: ZAR296 million).
  • Cartrack total revenue increased 16% to ZAR1,110 million in Q4 2025.

data.title: DXC Technology Non-GAAP EPS of $0.84 beats by $0.07, revenue of $3.17B beats by $40M
data.description:
• DXC Technology press release (NYSE:DXC): FQ4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.84 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $3.17B (-6.5% Y/Y) beats by $40M.
  • Shares +0.48%.
  • Q4 FY25 EBIT margin of 11.0%, and adjusted EBIT(2) margin of 7.3%
  • Q4 FY25 Diluted earnings per share was $1.43 vs. ($1.10) in the prior year quarter
  • Q4 FY25 Book to bill of 1.22x

  • Full Year Fiscal 2026 and First Quarter Fiscal Year 2026 Guidance

    Full Year Fiscal 2026

    • Total revenue in the range of $12.18 billion and $12.44 billion, a decline of 5.0% to 3.0% on an organic basis(1).
    • Adjusted EBIT margin(2) of 7.0% to 8.0%.
    • Non-GAAP diluted EPS(3) of $2.75 to $3.25.
    • Free Cash Flow(4) of ~$600 million.

    First Quarter Fiscal 2026

    • Total revenue in the range of $3.04 billion and $3.09 billion, a decline of 5.5% to 4.0% year-over-year on an organic basis.(1)
    • Adjusted EBIT margin(2) of 6.0% to 7.0%.
    • Non-GAAP Diluted EPS(3) of $0.55 to $0.65.
    • Q1 EPS consensus of $0.77
    • Q1 revenue consensus of $3.12B
    • FY26 EPS consensus of $3.41
    • FY26 revenue consensus of $12.41B

data.title: Acuren Corporation to uplist to New York Stock Exchange from NYSE American
data.description:
• Acuren Corporation (NYSE:TIC) has been approved for uplisting to the New York Stock Exchange from the NYSE American.
  • TIC’s common stock will cease trading on the NYSE American after market close on May 16, 2025, and will commence trading on the NYSE effective at the opening of trading on May 19, 2025.
  • TIC’s common stock will continue to trade under the symbol “TIC.”
  • Source: Press release
